title: Multi-Tenant Business Management SaaS
subtitle: End-to-End Platform for Trade Businesses
overview: Full-stack SaaS platform replacing fragmented CRM, estimation, scheduling, and invoicing tools for trade businesses.
architectureHighlights: Multi-tenant SaaS architecture with row-level PostgreSQL isolation
Dual pricing computation engine (Hourly & OPEX models)
Snapshot-based audit model for financial integrity

caseStudy.problem: Trade businesses rely on 4-5 disconnected tools for CRM, estimation, scheduling, and invoicing — causing duplicate data entry, poor workflow visibility, and manual coordination across teams.
caseStudy.solution: Designed and built a unified multi-tenant SaaS platform that manages the entire business lifecycle from lead capture to job completion and invoicing.
myRole: Led architecture design and backend development
Implemented RBAC and subscription plan enforcement
caseStudy.architecture:
graph TD
    A[Client App] --> B[API Gateway / NestJS]
    B --> C[Auth Module - JWT + RBAC]
    B --> D[Tenant Middleware]
    D --> E[CRM Module]
    D --> F[Estimation Engine]
    D --> G[Job & Work Order Module]
    D --> H[Scheduling Module]
    D --> I[Invoicing Module]
    D --> J[Subscription Module]
    E --> K[(PostgreSQL - Tenant Isolated)]
    F --> K
    G --> K
    H --> K
    I --> K
    J --> K

technicalDecisions.reasoning: Simpler ops than schema-per-tenant at 100-150 org scale
Each domain is an independent module — microservice-ready
Every request passes through tenant context middleware
Interceptors check seat limits and feature access before processing

challenges: Estimation engine complexity — Surface-based calculations with variable dimensions, coat counts, productivity rates, and material costs. Built a flexible formula engine supporting manual overrides.
Multi-tenant data isolation — Zero data leakage between 100+ organizations while keeping query performance fast.
RBAC at scale — Granular permissions across 14+ modules with role inheritance.
Subscription enforcement — Runtime checks for seat limits, feature gating, and plan upgrades without disrupting active users.
